title: racosperma viscidulum
intro: Racosperma viscidulum, also known as the sticky pea, is a plant species native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Racosperma viscidulum include its ability to treat various ailments such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by boiling its leaves and stems, which can also be used to make a poultice or salve for topical applications.
